info.name: Anomaly Detection API
info.description:
Complete API collection for the Banking Transaction Anomaly Detection System.

Includes all CRUD endpoints, evaluation scenarios for each of the 16 anomaly rules (including Temporal Rule Correlation), configuration management, silence detection, analytics with time filtering, AI chatbot (natural language queries), AI-powered transaction explanations with operator feedback, and demo data generation.

**Quick Start:** Hit `POST /api/v1/demo/generate` to populate the system with realistic data.

**Base URL:** http://localhost:8080

**Ollama (optional):** Run `ollama serve` and `ollama pull llama3.2:1b` for AI chatbot and transaction explanations.

item.name: Demo Data
item.description: Generate realistic demo data after startup. Evaluates ~166 transactions across 10 scenarios, populates review queue, submits feedback, and fires Prometheus metrics.

item.name: Generate Demo Data
item.description: Generates ~166 transactions: 125 warm-up (builds profiles) + 41 anomalous (triggers rules). Then submits feedback on ~60% of pending review items. Returns summary with PASS/ALERT/BLOCK counts.

item.name: Update Rule - Set Dormancy to 1 min (for testing)
Get Evaluation Result by Txn ID

request.description: Returns the anomaly evaluation result for a transaction. If Ollama is running, includes an `aiExplanation` field with a human-readable explanation of why the transaction was flagged (generated on first view, cached for subsequent requests).

item.name: Evaluate - Rule Trigger Scenarios
item.description: Each request is crafted to trigger a specific anomaly rule based on seeded client profiles. Run in order for best results — some rules (like Rapid Repeat and New Bene Velocity) require multiple sequential requests.

item.name: Rule 1: Transaction Type Anomaly
item.description: CLIENT-005 only uses IMPS/UPI. Sending RTGS triggers TRANSACTION_TYPE_ANOMALY (0% type frequency < 5% threshold).

item.name: Rule 2: TPS Spike (send 1 of 20)
item.description: CLIENT-003 averages ~2 txns/hour. Sending 20 in rapid succession triggers TPS_SPIKE. Run this request 20 times quickly (or use Postman Runner with 20 iterations).

item.name: Rule 3: Amount Anomaly (large single txn)
item.description: CLIENT-001 averages ~50K per txn. Sending 1M (20x normal) triggers AMOUNT_ANOMALY with ~100 partial score.

item.name: Rule 4: Hourly Amount Anomaly (setup 1/3)
item.description: CLIENT-002 averages ~2K/txn, ~40K/hour. Sending 3x 500K within the same hour triggers HOURLY_AMOUNT_ANOMALY. Send this 3 times.

item.name: Rule 5: Amount Per Type Anomaly
item.description: CLIENT-001 averages ~50K for NEFT. Sending 750K NEFT triggers AMOUNT_PER_TYPE_ANOMALY (15x type average).

item.name: Rule 6: Isolation Forest (multi-dimensional)
item.description: CLIENT-001 normally does NEFT with ~50K amounts. Sending a rare type (IFT) + unusual amount + unknown beneficiary creates multi-feature anomaly for the Isolation Forest ML model.

item.name: Rule 7: Beneficiary Rapid Repeat (send 1 of 6)
item.description: Sends to same beneficiary repeatedly within 1 hour. Send this 6 times to trigger BENEFICIARY_RAPID_REPEAT (threshold = 5). Use Postman Runner with 6 iterations.

item.name: Rule 8: Beneficiary Concentration
item.description: CLIENT-004 has 21 beneficiaries. Sending 30+ txns to the same beneficiary concentrates volume and triggers BENEFICIARY_CONCENTRATION. Send this request ~30 times.

item.name: Rule 9: Bene Amount Repetition (send 1 of 4)
item.description: Sends near-identical amounts (49999) to same beneficiary. After 3+ txns with CV < 10%, triggers BENEFICIARY_AMOUNT_REPETITION (threshold evasion). Send 4 times.

item.name: Rule 10: Daily Cumulative Amount (send 1 of 5)
item.description: CLIENT-002 has EWMA daily amount ~914K. With 150% variance, threshold ~2.3M. Send 5x 600K (total 3M) to trigger DAILY_CUMULATIVE_AMOUNT. Send 5 times.

item.name: Rule 11: New Bene Velocity (send 1 of 6)
item.description: Hard threshold = 5 new beneficiaries/day. Send 6 txns to 6 unique (never-seen) beneficiaries. Each request uses a random account number. Send 6 times.

item.name: Rule 12: Dormancy Reactivation
item.description: CLIENT-011 is a dormant client (2+ day gap since last activity). First update the dormancy rule to dormancyMinutes=1 using the 'Update Rule' request in the Rules folder, then send this. Triggers DORMANCY_REACTIVATION.

item.name: Rule 13: Cross-Channel Bene Amount (1/4 - NEFT)
item.description: Send 4x 1M to the SAME beneficiary via different txn types. CLIENT-004 threshold ~5.8M. Total will be 4M but combined with Rule 10 daily accumulation it may trigger. For guaranteed trigger use CLIENT-002 (threshold ~2.3M). Step 1 of 4.

item.name: Rule 13: Cross-Channel Bene Amount (2/4 - RTGS)
item.description: Step 2: Same beneficiary, different channel (RTGS).

item.name: Rule 13: Cross-Channel Bene Amount (3/4 - IMPS)
item.description: Step 3: Same beneficiary, different channel (IMPS).

item.name: Rule 13: Cross-Channel Bene Amount (4/4 - UPI trigger)
item.description: Step 4: Final channel. Total to XCHAN-TARGET-001 = 3.2M across NEFT/RTGS/IMPS/UPI. Exceeds CLIENT-002 threshold of ~2.3M. Triggers CROSS_CHANNEL_BENEFICIARY_AMOUNT.

item.name: Rule 14: Seasonal Deviation (send 1 of 25 at 3 AM)
item.description: CLIENT-001 has strong business-hours profile (90% of txns during 09-17 UTC). Sending 25+ transactions at 3 AM triggers SEASONAL_DEVIATION — hourly TPS at 3 AM far exceeds the seasonal baseline for that slot. Use Postman Runner with 25 iterations, or set your system clock to 3 AM UTC.

item.name: Rule 15: Mule Network — CLIENT-007 to shared beneficiary
item.description: CLIENT-007, CLIENT-008, and CLIENT-009 all send to 7 shared 'mule' beneficiaries. Sending a transaction from CLIENT-007 to one of these shared beneficiaries triggers MULE_NETWORK — fan-in=3, shared bene ratio >20%, network density=1.0.

item.name: Evaluate - Normal Transactions (PASS)
item.description: Transactions within normal behavioral patterns — expected to PASS with low or zero risk scores.

item.name: Review Queue & Feedback
item.description: Ops review queue for ALERT/BLOCK transactions. Provides feedback (True Positive / False Positive), bulk actions, AI explanation feedback (helpful/not helpful), queue stats, and rule weight adjustment history.

item.name: Get Queue Stats
item.description: Returns counts by feedback status: pending, truePositive, falsePositive, autoAccepted.

item.name: List Queue Items (all)
item.description: List all review queue items. Supports filters: action (ALERT/BLOCK), clientId, fromDate, toDate, ruleId, limit.

item.name: List Queue Items (time-filtered, last 24h)
item.description: Filter queue items by time range. fromDate and toDate are epoch milliseconds. This example uses a pre-script to compute last 24 hours.

item.name: List Queue Items (ALERT only)
item.description: Filter queue to show only ALERT actions.

item.name: List Queue Items (BLOCK only)
item.description: Filter queue to show only BLOCK actions.

item.name: List Queue Items (by Client)
item.description: Filter queue items by client ID.

item.name: Get Queue Item Detail
item.description: Returns composite detail: queue item + evaluation result + transaction + client profile. Replace TRIGGER-AMTTYPE-001 with an actual queued txnId.

request.description: Returns full detail: queue item + evaluation result (with AI explanation) + transaction + client profile. The `aiExplanation` field in the evaluation is generated by the Ollama LLM on first view and cached in Aerospike.

item.name: Submit Feedback - True Positive
item.description: Mark a queued transaction as a confirmed anomaly (True Positive). Replace the txnId in the URL.

item.name: Submit Feedback - False Positive
item.description: Dismiss a queued transaction as a false alarm (False Positive). Replace the txnId in the URL.

item.name: Bulk Feedback - True Positive
item.description: Mark multiple transactions as True Positive in one call. Update the txnIds array with actual queued IDs.

item.name: Submit AI Explanation Feedback (Helpful)
item.description: Rate the AI-generated explanation for a flagged transaction as helpful. Replace the txnId in the URL.

item.name: Submit AI Explanation Feedback (Not Helpful)
item.description: Rate the AI-generated explanation as not helpful. Replace the txnId in the URL.

item.name: Get AI Explanation Feedback
item.description: Retrieve existing feedback for a transaction's AI explanation. Returns 404 if no feedback submitted yet.

item.name: Get Weight History (all rules)
item.description: Returns recent rule weight adjustments from the auto-tuning job.

item.name: Get Weight History (specific rule)
item.description: Filter weight history for a specific rule ID.

item.name: Graph Status
item.description: Returns graph metadata: total beneficiaries, total clients, last refresh time, and whether the graph is ready.

item.name: Beneficiary Fan-In — Mule Beneficiary
item.description: Returns fan-in count and sender list for a known mule beneficiary. Expect fanIn=3 with senders CLIENT-007, CLIENT-008, CLIENT-009.

item.name: Beneficiary Fan-In — Normal Beneficiary
item.description: Returns fan-in for a normal beneficiary. Expect fanIn=1 (only one sender).

item.name: Client Graph Metrics — Mule Client
item.description: Returns shared beneficiary count, ratio, and network density for CLIENT-007 (mule network participant). Expect high shared % and density=1.0.

item.name: Client Graph Metrics — Normal Client
item.description: Returns graph metrics for CLIENT-001 (normal behavior). Expect low/zero shared beneficiaries and low density.

item.name: Analytics
item.description: Rule performance analytics, AI explanation feedback stats, and network visualization. Rule performance supports optional fromDate/toDate (epoch ms) for time-filtered stats.

item.name: AI Explanation Feedback Stats
item.description: Returns aggregate counts of helpful vs not helpful AI explanation ratings from operators.

item.name: Rule Performance Stats (all time)
item.description: Returns per-rule TP/FP counts and precision based on review queue feedback. Without time params, includes all feedback data.

item.name: Rule Performance Stats (time-filtered, last 24h)
item.description: Returns rule performance filtered by time range. Uses pre-request script to compute last 24 hours.

item.name: Client Network — Mule Client
item.description: Returns nodes and edges for beneficiary network visualization centered on CLIENT-007 (mule network participant). Expect shared beneficiaries with fan-in=3 and neighbor clients CLIENT-008/009.

item.name: Client Network — Normal Client
item.description: Returns network graph for CLIENT-001 (normal behavior). Expect all beneficiaries with fan-in=1 and no neighbor clients.

item.name: Client Risk Narrative — AI Generated
item.description: Uses the LLM to generate a plain-English behavioral summary and risk assessment for the specified client. Considers transaction patterns, rule trigger history, and profile stats. Returns {narrative, clientId}. Returns 404 if client profile not found. Falls back gracefully if Ollama is unavailable.

item.name: Configuration
item.description: Runtime configuration management. All changes apply immediately but reset to application.yml defaults on restart.

item.name: Get Risk Thresholds
item.description: Returns alertThreshold, blockThreshold, ewmaAlpha, minProfileTxns.

item.name: Update Risk Thresholds
item.description: Update global thresholds. alertThreshold must be < blockThreshold. ewmaAlpha must be in (0, 1).

item.name: Get Feedback Config
item.description: Returns auto-accept timeout, tuning interval, weight floor/ceiling, and max adjustment percentage.

item.name: Update Feedback Config
item.description: Update feedback and auto-tuning parameters. weightFloor must be < weightCeiling.

item.name: Get Transaction Types
item.description: Returns the list of supported transaction types (e.g., NEFT, RTGS, IMPS, UPI, IFT).

item.name: Update Transaction Types
item.description: Replace the transaction types list. Must contain at least one type. Types are uppercased and deduplicated.

item.name: Get Silence Detection Config
item.description: Returns silence detection settings: enabled, checkIntervalMinutes, silenceMultiplier, minExpectedTps, minCompletedHours.

item.name: Update Silence Detection Config
item.description: Update silence detection parameters. Multiplier and interval must be > 0.

item.name: Get Aerospike Info (read-only)
item.description: Returns Aerospike connection details: host, port, namespace.

item.name: Silence Detection
item.description: Monitor clients whose transaction flow has unexpectedly stopped. Enriched with profile data: EWMA TPS, expected gap, silence ratio.

item.name: Get Silent Clients
item.description: Returns currently silent clients with enriched profile data: ewmaHourlyTps, expectedGapMinutes, thresholdMinutes, silenceRatio, totalTxnCount, ewmaAmount.

item.name: Trigger Manual Silence Check
item.description: Runs an immediate silence detection scan and returns updated silent client list.

item.name: AI Chat
item.description: Natural language query interface powered by Ollama LLM with keyword-based fast parsing fallback.
